$comment: BD-FIXTURES — recorded zero-key SCRAPER responses (release-spec §1.1 Content Scraping / §1.2 Market Scraping; RD-9 scraping is BYO — never bundled creds; RD-12 the external call is dependency-injectable so CI holds no secrets). Keyed by `${platform}:${handle}` (handle lowercased, leading @ stripped). The fake scraper (tests/helpers/fake-scraper-adapter.js) replays these arrays of corpus-item.schema.json items INSTEAD of calling a real provider, so the ingestion+analysis flow runs with ZERO keys and ZERO network. Every item is Zone U (untrusted-scraped) and trust-class-tagged at write per RD-8; the engine writes them under $CONTENT_HOME/corpora/<brand>/. These recordings MIRROR the on-disk fixture corpora under corpora/acme-cosmos/ (own + competitors) so a test can compare the scraped path and the manual-submission path against the SAME expected analysis. `twitter:empty` is a recorded EMPTY scrape for the degrade-to-cold-start path (DD-21). An unrecorded key THROWS in the fake (a test asking for an unrecorded scrape is a test bug, not a silent pass). ALL SYNTHETIC — Acme Cosmos + invented competitors only. See PROVENANCE.md.

twitter:acmecosmos.text: Introducing the ST-1 first-light walkthrough in the app. Six taps: pick tonight's object, align the red dot, find, focus, look, save. Box open to a real object in under five minutes.
Blurry Moon? Two fixes, in this order. Swap to the 25mm eyepiece and center it, then nudge focus slowly past sharp and back. The crisp point is narrow and easy to overshoot.
Clear sky tonight? Point low at the eastern horizon after 9. The steady dot that doesn't twinkle is Jupiter. With the 25mm eyepiece you'll catch up to four of its moons in a tidy little line.
Drove an hour for dark sky and the clouds rolled in at 9:58. We still caught fifteen minutes on the Pleiades through the gap. Faint counts.
We ship a 25mm eyepiece as the default, not a high-power one. Wide and bright first means you actually find the thing. The 10mm is in the box for when you're ready to zoom in.
First time my kid found Saturn's rings on their own. The pause, then the quiet 'whoa.' That's the whole reason we built this.
Three minutes to a sharp Moon, every time: tabletop down, red-dot on the Moon, 25mm in, focus slow, then swap to the 10mm once it's centered.
Astronomy, not astrology. We will never tell you what the stars mean for your week. We'll tell you which one is a planet and how to point at it.

twitter:stellaroptics.text: 🚨 BIGGEST telescope launch of the YEAR is coming. You are NOT ready. Soon. 👀
Only 50 units left. Last chance before the window closes forever. Don't miss the drop of a lifetime.
Introducing the NovaMax 9000 — now live. The most revolutionary scope ever made, period.
GIVEAWAY! 🎉 RT + tag three friends to win a NovaMax. Winner picked tomorrow. Wen prize? Soon.

twitter:orbitoutfitters.text: How to find the Andromeda Galaxy in 4 steps: 1) face the Square of Pegasus, 2) hop to Mirach, 3) two stars up, 4) scan slow with low power. The smudge is a trillion suns.
The terminator is where the craters live. Aim at the day-night line, not the bright full face, for the sharpest shadows.
Saturn through our refractor last night. No caption needed.
On collimation: most beginners overthink it. A thirty-second laser check before each session is all a starter scope needs. Here's why the fear is overblown.
